data.title: Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58, revenue of $222.4M
data.description:
• Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. press release (PXED): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58.
  • Revenue of $222.4M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA was $34.8 million for second quarter 2026, compared to $32.3 million for second quarter 2025, and adjusted diluted earnings per share was $0.58 for the second quarter 2026, compared to $0.56 for second quarter 2025.2

data.title: Levi Strauss & Co. CFGO Harmit Singh to retire, successor search begins
data.description:
• Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) announced on Tuesday that Executive Vice President and Chief Financial & Growth Officer (CFGO) Harmit Singh will continue in his role as CFGO until a successor is appointed and then transition to serve as Special Advisor, following which he will retire.
  • The company said it has initiated a comprehensive search process with the assistance of an executive search firm.
  • The company stated that Singh will continue to serve as CFGO until a successor is appointed and will remain for a planned transition as Special Advisor to ensure continuity.
  • Singh joined LS&Co. in 2013 as Chief Financial Officer, taking responsibility for the company’s global finance, information technology, M&A, investor relations, strategic sourcing, and global business services functions. In 2023, his role expanded to include chief growth officer, where he helped shape its corporate strategy, accelerate transformation initiatives, and advance several key enablers of our future, including global real estate, franchise expansion, and the development of our Global Talent Hubs. Before joining LS&Co., Singh served as chief financial officer at Hyatt Hotels Corporation and held division CFO roles at Yum! Restaurants International and Pizza Hut.
  • LEVI +6.03% after hours to $20.9. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Scorpio Tankers announces proposed offering of convertible senior notes
data.description:
• Scorpio Tankers (STNG) intends to offer $300M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private offering.
  • The company also expects to grant to the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $45M aggregate principal amount of notes.
  • The company expects to use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering to repurchase shares of its common stock concurrently with the closing of the offering. 
  • The company intends to use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering to repurchase shares of common stock as described above and the remainder of the net proceeds for general corporate purposes.
  • STNG +0.23% after hours to $74.36.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Levi Strauss Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42, revenue of $1.7B
data.description:
• Levi Strauss press release (LEVI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42.
  • Revenue of $1.7B.
  • Operating margin was 11.4% in Q1 2026 compared to 12.5% in Q1 2025. Adjusted EBIT margin was 12.5% in Q1 2026 compared to 13.4% in Q1 2025, reflecting the impact of tariffs and planned increases in advertising.
    • Gross margin was 61.9% compared to 62.1% in Q1 2025 primarily due to the impact of tariffs, partially offset by price increases and less promotional activity.
    • Selling, general and administrative (SG&A) expenses were $872 million compared to $749 million in Q1 2025. Adjusted SG&A expenses were up 15.7% to $861 million compared to $744 million last year primarily due to the planned higher advertising costs in connection with the launch of the Behind Every Original campaign, the higher-than-expected sales volume and foreign exchange.
  • Interest and other income (expense), net, which includes foreign exchange gains and losses, were income of $30 million and expenses of $15 million in the aggregate in Q1 2026 and Q1 2025, respectively.
  • The effective income tax rate was 22.4%, compared to 20.6% in Q1 2025.
  • Net income from continuing operations was $177 million compared to $140 million in Q1 2025. Adjusted net income was $167 million compared to $150 million in Q1 2025.
  • Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was $0.45 compared to $0.35 in Q1 2025. Adjusted diluted earnings per share was $0.42 compared to $0.38 in Q1 2025.
  •

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq crawl out of red territory as Iran is reportedly reviewing two-week ceasefire deal
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed mixed on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 ((SP500)) and the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) ending higher after reports that Pakistan is mediating a two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed +0.1%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished -0.2%.

“Markets are on edge as the U.S. deadline for a deal with Iran approaches tonight. Most markets are lower, but energy prices continue to move higher,” Seeking Alpha analyst Andrew Hecht said.

“Expect substantial volatility over the coming days as surprises seem likely. The bottom line is that the markets are in Middle East risk-off mode going into tonight and the deadline, with tremendous uncertainty over the coming days. Be extremely cautious in markets across all asset classes,” he added.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 3 basis points lower at 4.31%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 4 basis points to 3.82%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were slightly lower at $112, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $107 per barrel.

“A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again. I don’t want that to happen, but it probably will,” Trump said on Truth Social, ahead of the 8 p.m. deadline he set for the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

“We will find out tonight, one of the most important moments in the long and complex history of the world. 47 years of extortion, corruption, and death will finally end,” he wrote.

In addition, Iran had allegedly responded by cutting off communication with the U.S.

Earlier, the U.S. has reportedly attacked military targets on Kharg Island as the 8 p.m. ET deadline arrives. According to media reports, electricity supply was disrupted in parts of the Iranian island after projectiles struck transmission lines.

In economic news, durable goods orders fell more than expected in February.

Consumers' inflation expectations increased short- and medium-term and held unchanged at the longer-term outlook, as gas price growth expectations jumped to the highest level since March 2022, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

U.S. consumer credit fell by $19.9B MoM in February, compared to a $6B sequential drop in January, according to the Federal Reserve.

data.title: Integrated Quantum Technologies launches non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Integrated Cyber Solutions (CSE: ICS) (OTCQB: IGCRF) on Tuesday said it will raise up to C$2 million through a non-brokered private placement of 4 million units at C$0.50 each.
  • Each unit will include one common share and half a warrant, exercisable at C$0.75 for two years.
  • Proceeds are intended for general working capital and software development.
  • The offering is expected to close on April 17, 2026, subject to Canadian Securities Exchange approvals.
  • The company also appointed Margaret Miller as corporate secretary, entering into a management services agreement with Branson Corporate Services Ltd., where Miller serves as a director.
  • IGCRF closed -5.26% at $0.4159.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Transportation Sec. Duffy says there's room for U.S. airline consolidation
data.description:
U.S. Transportation Sec. Sean Duffy said he believes there's room for consolidation in the U.S. airline sector. 

"Is there room for some mergers in the aviation industry? Yeah, I think there is," Duffy said in an interview with CNBC on Tuesday. 

Duffy's comments come after JetBlue (JBLU) shares soared 13% on March 25 after a Semafor report that the airline was evaluating plans to sell itself to a rival. JetBlue (JBLU)  tapped advisers to see how a deal with United Airlines (UAL), Alaska Airlines (ALK) or Southwest Airlines (LUV)might might be perceived in Washington.

The comments also come after a  federal judge blocked JetBlue's (JBLU) attempt to acquire Spirit Airlines (FLYYQ) in January 2024 on antitrust grounds.

data.title: Pakistan urges Trump to extend Iran deadline for two weeks
data.description:
Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Tuesday called on U.S. President Donald Trump to extend diplomatic negotiations by two weeks, as efforts to peacefully resolve the Middle East conflict continue to show promise.

In a social media post, Sharif described the diplomatic efforts as “progressing steadily, strongly and powerfully with the potential to lead to substantive results in near future.” He urged Trump to allow more time for diplomacy to run its course.

The Pakistani leader also appealed to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for a corresponding two-week period as a “goodwill gesture.” Additionally, Sharif called on all warring parties to observe a ceasefire everywhere during this period to enable diplomacy to achieve what he called a “conclusive termination of war.”

The appeal comes ahead of Trump's 8:00 PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, although timing is subject to change if talks keep progressing.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Exxon reiterated Buy at UBS on expected boost from helium supply disruption
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (XOM) was reiterated Tuesday at UBS with a Buy rating and $171 price target,which cited mounting concerns over global helium supply disruptions caused by Iran's drone and missile strikes on Qatar, which accounts for ~31% of global helium production.

The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to Western commercial shipping, and helium must be transported in specialized cryogenic ISO containers by sea, leaving no viable export route for Middle Eastern supply to reach outside markets without significant delays.

UBS analyst Manav Gupta said the disruption in helium markets will provide a significant boost to Exxon Mobil (XOM), whose LaBarge facility in Wyoming provides 20% of the world's helium supply.

"With over 30% of global capacity disrupted, this location will play a key role in meeting global needs for helium, which is a critical element for many advanced technologies, like MRIs for healthcare, rockets for space exploration, and microchips for advanced computing," Gupta wrote.

Exxon's (XOM) position gives it upside to pricing and advantages of supply security compared with certain industrial gas majors that rely on Qatar for production, the analyst said.

data.title: ‘Oil above $100/bbl is a tried-and-true recession trigger’ – analyst
data.description:
Victoria Greene, founding partner and CIO at G Squared Private Wealth, is shifting to a more defensive market posture as oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) cross the $100 per barrel threshold—a level she identifies as a critical warning sign for the economy.

In an interview with CNBC, Greene explained that her firm is actively “pulling back a little bit on risk” to prepare for potential market volatility amid growing geopolitical tensions.

The analyst pointed to historical economic cycles as part of her rationale, noting that recessions typically occur every 6.25 years and the last one was in 2020.

“Oil (CL1:COM) above a $100 a barrel is a tried-and-true recession trigger,” Greene said. “When you start piling these things together, you can make an argument that a potential recession could happen if this continues to drag on.”

Greene highlighted the “massive asymmetrical risk” introduced by regional conflicts involving Iran, Israel, and Gulf allies that could lead to overnight escalations.

While her base case remains that the conflict will be resolved, she emphasized that investors need to consider what happens if the situation intensifies dramatically.

“We’re not the only party in this situation anymore,” she noted, explaining that coordinating four parties with different goals increases uncertainty.

G Squared is reducing exposure to emerging markets (EEM), particularly in Asia (AIA), (ASEA) and Europe (VGK), (IEV), (IEUR), while increasing holdings in energy (XLE) and real assets.

The firm favors sectors including aerospace and defense (ITA), (PPA), (XAR), (SHLD), and cybersecurity (CIBR), (HACK), (IHAK), with names like Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), Palo Alto (PANW), and CrowdStrike (CRWD) among their picks.

Greene suggested investors could also see a “flight to quality” into consumer staples (XLP) and healthcare (XLV) while maintaining core technology positions.

The IEA’s recent short-term outlook reported approximately 9.1M barrels per day of Middle Eastern supply now shut in, representing significant disruption that will require extensive infrastructure rebuilding.

Greene expressed concern that rising diesel and gasoline prices (XB1:COM), combined with upcoming CPI data, could become a major “headwind” for consumers and erode economic catalysts like tax refunds.

Despite the cautious repositioning, Greene stressed that her firm is not abandoning the market entirely.

“We’re not wholesale fire selling. We’re not saying sit 30% in cash,” she said. “We’re not sitting here saying the sky is falling. We’re just saying we need to be prepared.”
